policy_name: Post Graduate Indira Gandhi Scholarship For Single Girl Child

sections.overview: A scholarship scheme by the University Grants Commission for girl students who are the only girl child in the family and are admitted in the first year of postgraduate courses. It provides financial assistance of ₹36,200 per year for the duration of the PG course.
sections.objective: To support postgraduate education of single girl children and promote the value of small family norms.
eligibility: Applicant must be a citizen of India
Applicant must be the only girl child in the family
Twin or fraternal daughters are eligible
Age must not exceed 30 years at the time of admission
Must be admitted to a regular full-time first-year postgraduate course
Must be pursuing a non-professional course in a recognized institution
Distance education mode is not allowed
Applicable only for first-year PG students
benefits: ₹36,200 per annum for a period of two years
No additional benefits for hostel, medical, or other expenses
application_process: Verify mobile number and complete e-KYC
Generate OTR ID using NSP OTR App and AadhaarFaceRD App
Fill application form and upload documents
documents_required: Proof of admission in a recognized PG course
Affidavit for single girl child status
exclusions: Distance education PG courses are not eligible
Institutions not funded by Central or State Government are not eligible
Applicants with a brother in the family are not eligible

faq.question: Should the candidate select scholarship or incentive?
faq.answer: The candidate must select 'incentive' while applying.

faq.question: Is a girl with a brother eligible?
faq.answer: No, only single girl children are eligible, except twin daughters.

faq.question: Can candidates above 30 apply?
faq.answer: Yes, if they were below 30 at admission or application deadline.

faq.question: Is distance mode PG eligible?
faq.answer: No, distance education is not allowed.

faq.question: Are hostel or medical expenses covered?
faq.answer: No, only the scholarship amount is provided.

faq.question: Is tuition fee reimbursed?
faq.answer: No, tuition fee reimbursement is not allowed.

faq.question: Can second-year PG students apply?
faq.answer: No, only first-year PG students can apply.

faq.question: Can this be availed with other scholarships?
faq.answer: Yes, it can be availed along with other scholarships.
